title: Substract duration from datetime
description:
I want to get the date of yesterday (actually for filtering) and identified a way to compute the date of yesterday in Virtuoso by subtracting an integer of seconds from the current timestamp:
SELECT DISTINCT (xsd:date(NOW()) AS ?today) (xsd:date(NOW()-(3600*24)) AS ?yesterday)
WHERE {?s a ?o.}

Apache Jena does not support subtracting integers from xsd:date/xsd:datetime, it demands a time period as xsd:duration, which is much more intuitive, e.g.
SELECT DISTINCT (xsd:date(NOW()) AS ?today) (xsd:date(NOW()-"P1D"^^xsd:duration) AS ?yesterday)
WHERE {?s a ?o.}

That again is not supported by Virtuoso. Would be great to have compatible queries for this.
poc:
SELECT DISTINCT (xsd:date(NOW()) AS ?today) (xsd:date(NOW()-(3600*24)) AS ?yesterday)
WHERE {?s a ?o.}

SELECT DISTINCT (xsd:date(NOW()) AS ?today) (xsd:date(NOW()-"P1D"^^xsd:duration) AS ?yesterday)
WHERE {?s a ?o.}
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT PRIMARY KEY, event_date xsd:date NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_date) VALUES (1, xsd:date(NOW())), (2, xsd:date(NOW() - (3600 * 24))), (3, xsd:date(NOW() - (3600 * 48)));
SELECT id, event_date, xsd:date(NOW() - (3600 * 24)) AS yesterday FROM test_dates WHERE event_date < xsd:date(NOW());
SELECT id, event_date FROM test_dates WHERE event_date = xsd:date(NOW() - (3600 * 24)) OR event_date IS NULL;
SELECT DISTINCT (xsd:date(NOW()) AS ?today), (xsd:date(NOW() - (3600 * 24)) AS ?yesterday) WHERE { ?s a ?o . };
